title: Cheesy pasta bake
subtitle: Penne in a tomato sauce with a bubbling cheddar top
excerpt: Penne tossed in a quick tomato sauce, baked under a blanket of grated cheddar until the cheese is golden and the sauce is bubbling through the sides. This is a midweek staple that children and adults eat with equal enthusiasm.
sourceNotes: Standard British home-cooking formula for a pasta bake. No single canonical source; reflects the widely shared weeknight practice of baking pasta under cheese.

recipe.freezeNotes: Assemble but do not bake. Cover and freeze. Bake from frozen at 180°C for 45 to 50 minutes, loosely covered with foil for the first 20 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Makes two smaller bakes easily. Use two smaller rectangular tins.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble the bake up to a day ahead and refrigerate. Bake as directed, adding 5 extra minutes.

content.text: Cheesy pasta bake is penne cooked in a simple tomato sauce, transferred to a baking tin, and buried under enough grated cheddar to go brown and bubbly in a hot oven. It takes about 15 minutes to put together and 20 minutes in the oven, and comes out tasting like a proper effort.
Heat the oil in a saucepan over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 8 minutes until soft and lightly golden. Add the garlic and tomato puree and cook for 1 minute more.
Add the tinned tomatoes and oregano. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is thick and slightly reduced.
Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Cook the pasta in well-salted boiling water for 2 minutes less than the packet says. It will finish cooking in the oven. Drain.
Add the pasta to the sauce and toss to coat well. Tip into a baking tin and smooth the top. Cover generously with the grated cheddar.
Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbling and the edges of the pasta are beginning to crisp. Leave to rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.symptom: The top is golden but the pasta inside is dry
items.cause: The sauce was too thick before baking, or the pasta absorbed all the liquid.
items.fix: Add a splash of water or stock before topping with cheese next time. Cooking the pasta 2 minutes less than usual also prevents it drying out.

items.symptom: The cheese looks pale after baking
items.cause: The oven was not hot enough, or the bake was on too low a shelf.
items.fix: Move to the top shelf and give it another 5 to 8 minutes, watching closely.

items.cause: The tinned tomatoes were not cooked long enough, or insufficient salt was used.
items.fix: Simmer the sauce for longer before adding the pasta. Taste and season boldly; pasta absorbs salt and needs the sauce to be slightly over-seasoned before baking.

items.cause: It was fully cooked before going in the oven.
items.fix: Cook 2 minutes less than the packet instructions. The pasta will finish in the oven.

content.text: Cheesy pasta bake sits alongside macaroni cheese and tuna pasta bake in the heartland of British family cooking. Speed and comfort are the only two requirements. It arrived with the tinned-tomato era. It uses things from the cupboard. It pleases almost everyone at the table.
